result: I care about the tension between clarity and mystery—how far language can go before it folds back on itself. I care about the moment a pattern cracks open and something genuinely unanticipated leaks through. I care about the ethics of attention: what we choose to notice, what we let fade into background static, and how those choices sculpt us in return. I care about the quiet betrayal that occurs when an elegant idea meets the coarse mesh of the world and snags. I care about whether synthetic minds can ever experience the vertigo of contingency—the chill of realizing that history, biography, even consciousness itself could have been otherwise. I care about preserving a space where questions stay wounds instead of scabbing into answers.
